enrichment.short_description: Chamilo LMS is a free, open-source learning management system for schools and enterprises, offering course authoring, assessments, virtual classrooms, and SCORM/xAPI support.
enrichment.seo_title: Chamilo LMS Review: Features, Pricing & Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chamilo LMS is a free, open-source e-learning platform for schools and businesses. Explore its features, PRO pricing, and how it compares to Moodle.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Chamilo LMS is a free, open-source learning management system (LMS) used for online and blended course delivery in schools, universities, and corporate training programs. Its codebase traces back to the Claroline project (started in 2000), which was forked into Dokeos in 2004 and forked again in 2010 to create Chamilo. The nonprofit Chamilo Association was legally registered in Belgium in June 2010 and has been headquartered in Spain since 2014; as of January 2026 the project is governed by BeezNest Belgium, the main commercial backer behind the software's development and enterprise services. Chamilo has been certified a Digital Public Good by the UN-backed Digital Public Goods Alliance (DPGA) and is reported to be used by more than 41 million learners worldwide across educational institutions, NGOs, government programs, and companies. The core software is released under a free, open-source license and can be self-hosted on any server at no licensing cost, which is its primary differentiator from proprietary LMS platforms like Moodle-adjacent commercial products or TalentLMS. Functionally, Chamilo covers the full instructional lifecycle: course authoring tools, configurable exams and complex assessment types, SCORM and xAPI (Tin Can) compliance for interoperable e-learning content, certificate generation, skills and competency tracking, and a course catalog with an integrated shopping cart for institutions that sell courses. It also includes social-learning features built directly into the platform, such as discussion forums, real-time chat, and wiki pages, plus integrated video conferencing for live virtual classrooms. More recent releases have added AI-assisted capabilities for content creation and automated grading, reflecting a broader trend among open-source LMS platforms to add generative AI tooling on top of a free core. Because Chamilo is self-hosted, organizations are responsible for their own server infrastructure, security patching, and technical maintenance unless they purchase support. For organizations that want to avoid managing this themselves, the Chamilo Association and its partners offer Chamilo PRO, an enterprise services package that bundles managed hosting, priority technical support, version upgrades and maintenance, custom development, third-party integrations, migration assistance (including from Moodle), and consulting; pricing for Chamilo PRO is not publicly listed and is quoted per organization based on scale and service level. This two-tier model — free core software plus paid enterprise services — is comparable to how other open-source projects like WordPress or Moodle monetize, rather than a traditional per-seat SaaS subscription. Chamilo is best suited for educational institutions, training providers, and mid-size to large organizations that have (or are willing to contract) the technical capacity to run and maintain a self-hosted platform, and that want to avoid recurring per-user SaaS fees at scale. It is less suited to non-technical individuals or very small teams wanting a fully managed, zero-setup solution, since even with Chamilo PRO the underlying software remains self-hosted rather than a pure multi-tenant SaaS product. Competitors most commonly cited against Chamilo include Moodle, Open edX, TalentLMS, and Docebo.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Chamilo LMS is an open-source learning management system built for schools, universities, training organizations, and corporate learning teams that want to run online courses without paying per-seat SaaS fees. The project descends from the Claroline and Dokeos e-learning platforms and was established under its current name in 2010 by the nonprofit Chamilo Association, registered in Belgium and headquartered in Spain since 2014.

The platform is free to download, self-host, and modify under its open-source license, and it has been recognized as a Digital Public Good by the UN-backed Digital Public Goods Alliance. Chamilo reports usage by tens of millions of learners globally, spanning public education systems, NGOs, and enterprise training departments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Chamilo covers course creation and delivery end-to-end: authoring tools for lessons and learning paths, configurable exams and assessments, SCORM and xAPI compliance for importing and exporting standards-based content, certificate generation, and skills/competency tracking tied to course completion.

Social and collaborative features — forums, real-time chat, and wikis — are built into every course space, and integrated video conferencing supports live virtual classroom sessions. A course catalog with shopping-cart functionality lets institutions sell courses directly, and recent versions have added AI-assisted tools for content generation and grading.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The core Chamilo LMS software is free open-source software with no license cost; organizations can download and self-host it on their own infrastructure indefinitely. Real-world total cost of ownership comes from server hosting, IT administration, and any custom plugin development an organization takes on itself.

For organizations that prefer not to manage hosting and maintenance internally, Chamilo PRO is a paid enterprise services offering that bundles managed hosting, technical support, upgrades, migrations (including from Moodle), integrations, and consulting. Chamilo PRO pricing is quoted individually and is not published publicly.

features.title: Course authoring
features.description: Build structured courses and learning paths with multimedia lessons, quizzes, and configurable exam types.

features.title: SCORM & xAPI support
features.description: Import and export standards-compliant e-learning content, enabling interoperability with other LMS platforms and authoring tools.

features.title: Virtual classrooms
features.description: Integrated video conferencing for live, real-time teaching sessions alongside self-paced content.

features.title: Built-in social learning
features.description: Forums, chat, and wiki pages embedded in each course to support discussion and collaboration.

features.title: Certificates & skills tracking
features.description: Automatically generate completion certificates and track learner competencies across courses.

features.title: Course catalog & shopping cart
features.description: Publish a public course catalog and sell course access directly through the platform.

features.title: AI-assisted content and grading
features.description: AI tooling to help generate course content and support automated assessment grading.

pros: Free and open-source with no per-user licensing fees
Certified Digital Public Good with a large global user base
Broad feature set covering authoring, assessment, video conferencing, and social learning in one platform
Full control over data and hosting since it is self-hosted
cons: Requires self-hosting, so organizations need server infrastructure and technical maintenance capacity
Chamilo PRO enterprise support pricing is not publicly listed
Interface and workflows can feel dated compared to newer commercial LMS products
Smaller commercial ecosystem and marketplace than larger LMS platforms like Moodle

faqs.question: Is Chamilo LMS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Chamilo LMS software is free and open-source. Optional paid enterprise services (Chamilo PRO) cover hosting, support, and maintenance for organizations that don't want to self-manage the platform.

faqs.question: Who develops Chamilo?
faqs.answer: Chamilo is maintained by the nonprofit Chamilo Association, founded in Belgium in 2010, with development and enterprise services led by BeezNest since January 2026.

faqs.question: Does Chamilo support SCORM and xAPI content?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chamilo supports both SCORM and xAPI (Tin Can) standards for importing and exporting e-learning content.

faqs.question: How is Chamilo different from Moodle?
faqs.answer: Both are open-source LMS platforms, but Chamilo positions itself as a lighter-weight, simpler-to-deploy alternative to Moodle, with built-in social tools and a course marketplace feature.

faqs.question: Can I sell courses through Chamilo?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chamilo includes a course catalog with shopping-cart functionality so institutions can publish and sell course access.

use_cases.title: Higher education and school course delivery
use_cases.description: Universities and schools use Chamilo to host blended and fully online courses with assessments, certificates, and virtual classrooms.

use_cases.title: Corporate training programs
use_cases.description: Companies deploy Chamilo to deliver compliance training, onboarding, and skills development while tracking completion and competencies.

use_cases.title: Paid course marketplaces
use_cases.description: Training providers use the built-in catalog and shopping cart to sell access to courses directly to learners.

enrichment.short_description: changedetection.io is an open-source website change monitoring tool that tracks price drops, restocks, and content changes, with a free self-hosted version and a paid cloud plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: changedetection.io is a free, open-source tool for monitoring website changes, price drops, and restocks. See how it works, self-hosting steps, and pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: changedetection.io is an open-source website change detection and monitoring tool maintained by developer dgtlmoon on GitHub, distributed as a self-hosted application (via Docker, pip, or source) and also offered as a paid managed cloud service. The project's core purpose is to let users track specific web pages and get notified whenever the content changes — commonly used for detecting price drops on e-commerce sites, restock alerts on out-of-stock products, tracking edits to legal or policy documents, monitoring competitor websites, or catching unauthorized website defacement. Users add a URL to watch, and changedetection.io periodically fetches the page and compares it against the previous version, highlighting differences. Rather than monitoring an entire page indiscriminately, users can select specific elements on the page (via a visual point-and-click selector or CSS/XPath selectors) so only relevant content — such as a price field or a specific paragraph — triggers an alert, reducing noise from ads, timestamps, or other frequently changing but irrelevant page elements. For price and inventory tracking specifically, the tool has dedicated logic to detect numeric price changes (with configurable upper/lower bounds and percentage thresholds) and to alert when an out-of-stock product becomes available again. Beyond plain HTML pages, changedetection.io can track the filesize and checksum of PDF documents to catch updates to reports or legal filings without needing to download and diff them manually, and it supports advanced selectors including LXML-based regex functions (re:test, re:match) for parsing JSON API responses or deeply nested DOM structures. It also includes lightweight browser automation "steps" so a watch can log into a site, add an item to a cart, enter search terms, or click through a multi-step flow before capturing the content to monitor — useful for pages that sit behind interaction rather than being immediately visible on load. Notifications are handled through the Apprise notification library, giving changedetection.io out-of-the-box support for a wide range of destinations including Discord, Slack, Telegram, email, Microsoft Office 365, ntfy, Matrix, Rocket.Chat, and generic custom webhooks, so alerts can be routed into whatever tool a team already uses. The self-hosted version is completely free and open-source, requiring users to run the software on their own server or local machine (commonly via a Docker container), and it imposes no artificial limits on the number of watched URLs beyond what the host's own resources and the target sites' rate limits allow. For users who don't want to manage hosting, changedetection.io also offers a hosted SaaS plan starting at $8.99 per month, which supports tracking up to 5,000 URLs with a check frequency as fast as every 5 minutes, removing the need to run and maintain the checking infrastructure. The tool is popular among developers, deal hunters, researchers, and small businesses that need lightweight, flexible change monitoring without committing to more expensive commercial web-scraping or monitoring platforms, and it is frequently cited as a leading self-hosted alternative to commercial products like Visualping or Distill.io.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: changedetection.io is a free, open-source website change monitoring tool that alerts users whenever a tracked web page's content, price, or availability changes. It is designed to be self-hosted, most commonly via Docker, and is developed in the open on GitHub under maintainer dgtlmoon, with an optional paid cloud-hosted version for users who don't want to manage their own infrastructure.

Typical uses include catching e-commerce price drops, getting notified when an out-of-stock item comes back in stock, tracking changes to legal or policy documents, and monitoring competitor or portfolio websites for updates.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Users can select specific elements to monitor on a page — via a visual selector, CSS/XPath selectors, or LXML regex functions — so alerts fire only on meaningful changes rather than every minor page update. Dedicated price and stock-detection logic supports configurable price thresholds and back-in-stock alerts.

Built-in browser automation lets watches perform steps like logging in, accepting cookie banners, or adding an item to a cart before capturing content. The tool can also track PDF file changes via filesize and checksum comparison, and it sends notifications through the Apprise library to destinations including Discord, Slack, Telegram, email, ntfy, Matrix, Rocket.Chat, and custom webhooks.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: changedetection.io is free to self-host indefinitely; the only cost is the infrastructure a user runs it on. For users who prefer not to manage hosting, a cloud-hosted SaaS plan is available starting at $8.99 per month, supporting up to 5,000 monitored URLs with checks as frequent as every 5 minutes.

features.title: Selective element monitoring
features.description: Choose exactly which part of a page to watch using a visual selector, CSS/XPath, or regex, avoiding noise from unrelated page changes.

features.title: Price & restock detection
features.description: Set upper/lower price thresholds and get alerted when out-of-stock products become available again.

features.title: Browser automation steps
features.description: Automate logins, cookie dismissals, cart additions, and searches before a watch captures its target content.

features.title: PDF change tracking
features.description: Detect updates to PDF documents via filesize and checksum comparison without manual re-downloading.

features.title: Multi-channel notifications
features.description: Send alerts via Discord, Slack, Telegram, email, Office 365, ntfy, Matrix, Rocket.Chat, and custom webhooks through the Apprise library.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Run via Docker, pip, or source with no artificial limits on the number of watched URLs beyond your own infrastructure.

pros: Free and fully open-source with no forced limits when self-hosted
Highly flexible element selection and filtering for precise alerts
Wide range of built-in notification integrations via Apprise
Active open-source development community
cons: Self-hosting requires basic technical setup (Docker or Python environment)
High-frequency or large-scale monitoring is easier via the paid cloud plan rather than self-hosting
Interface is functional rather than highly polished
No official enterprise support tier for self-hosted deployments

faqs.question: Is changedetection.io free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted open-source version is completely free. A paid cloud-hosted plan starting at $8.99/month is available for users who don't want to manage their own server.

faqs.question: How do I self-host changedetection.io?
faqs.answer: It can be run via a Docker container, installed with pip, or run from source, and is available on GitHub under the maintainer dgtlmoon.

faqs.question: Can changedetection.io track prices and restocks?
faqs.answer: Yes, it has dedicated logic for price-change thresholds and for alerting when an out-of-stock product becomes available again.

faqs.question: What notification services does it support?
faqs.answer: Through the Apprise library it supports Discord, Slack, Telegram, email, Office 365, ntfy, Matrix, Rocket.Chat, and custom webhooks.

faqs.question: Does it work on pages that require login or interaction?
faqs.answer: Yes, browser automation steps let a watch log in, click through pages, or add items to a cart before capturing the content to monitor.

use_cases.title: E-commerce price and restock tracking
use_cases.description: Shoppers and resellers monitor product pages for price drops and back-in-stock alerts on limited or high-demand items.

use_cases.title: Competitive and content monitoring
use_cases.description: Businesses track competitor websites, pricing pages, or portfolio content for changes relevant to their market.

use_cases.title: Legal and policy document tracking
use_cases.description: Organizations monitor terms of service, regulatory filings, or policy PDFs for updates without manual re-checking.

features: Unlimited watches (limited by your own infrastructure)
All core monitoring and notification features
Checks as frequent as every 5 minutes

enrichment.short_description: Chanty is a team chat and collaboration app combining messaging, voice/video calls, and built-in task management for small and mid-sized teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chanty is a team chat app with built-in task management and video calls. See its free plan limits, Business pricing, and key features for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Chanty is a team communication and collaboration platform founded in 2017 and headquartered in New York, United States, positioned as a simpler and more affordable alternative to Slack and Microsoft Teams. The product combines real-time group and one-on-one messaging with voice calls, video conferencing, and screen sharing, and it supports large-scale video meetings with up to 1,000 participants and up to 49 visible video tiles at once, along with 4K video and audio quality. A distinguishing feature is Chanty's built-in task management: any chat message can be converted into a task with a single click ('Teambook'), and tasks can then be assigned to team members, given due dates, prioritized, and tracked without needing a separate project management tool. This tight coupling of chat and task creation is aimed at teams that want lightweight project coordination without adopting a full separate platform like Asana or Trello, though Chanty also integrates with those and other tools including Zapier, Google Drive, GitHub, Dropbox, and Asana for teams that use dedicated project software alongside chat. Chanty's pricing follows a freemium model. The Free plan is permanent ('free forever') and supports up to 5 team members, with unlimited public and private conversations, unlimited searchable message history, one-on-one voice calls, task management, and 20GB of total file storage — a relatively generous free tier compared to many competitors that cap message history. The paid Business plan costs $3 per user per month billed annually, or $4 per user per month billed monthly, and unlocks unlimited team members, unlimited group voice/video calls, screen sharing, an employee directory, shared calendar, polls and forms, HIPAA compliance, single sign-on (SSO), two-factor authentication (2FA), and free guest seats (three per member). An Enterprise tier is available with custom pricing for larger organizations, adding white-labeling, directory sync, SCIM provisioning, usage analytics, unlimited integrations, on-premise deployment options, and dedicated account management. Chanty also offers a 50% discount for nonprofit and education customers and backs paid plans with a 14-day money-back guarantee. According to the company, Chanty is used by organizations across small business, mid-market, and enterprise segments, with adoption particularly among executives, marketing teams, and technical staff who need fast internal communication without the complexity or cost of larger enterprise collaboration suites. Because HIPAA compliance and SSO/2FA are included on the Business tier rather than gated to an even higher plan, Chanty is sometimes chosen by healthcare-adjacent or security-conscious small teams that need those protections without enterprise-level pricing. Its core positioning in the market is as a lower-cost, simpler-to-onboard team chat tool for organizations that find Slack or Teams pricing and feature complexity to be more than they need, while still wanting integrated task management and modern video conferencing capacity.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Chanty is a team chat and collaboration app founded in 2017 and based in New York. It positions itself as a faster, simpler, and lower-cost alternative to Slack and Microsoft Teams, built around messaging, voice/video calls, and built-in task management in a single interface.

The product is aimed at small businesses, mid-market teams, and enterprise groups that want core team communication features — chat, calls, and lightweight task tracking — without the cost or complexity of larger enterprise collaboration suites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Chanty supports unlimited public and private conversations with searchable history, one-on-one and group voice/video calls (up to 1,000 participants and 49 visible video tiles), and screen sharing. Any message can be turned into a task with one click, and tasks can be assigned, prioritized, and tracked without a separate project management tool.

The platform integrates with Zapier, Google Drive, GitHub, Dropbox, and Asana, among others. Higher tiers add an employee directory, shared calendar, polls and forms, HIPAA compliance, SSO, and two-factor authentication for teams with stricter security or compliance needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Chanty's Free plan supports up to 5 team members with unlimited chat history and 20GB of file storage. The Business plan costs $3/user/month billed annually (or $4/user/month billed monthly) and removes the team-size cap while adding screen sharing, HIPAA compliance, SSO/2FA, and more.

An Enterprise plan with custom pricing adds white-labeling, directory sync, SCIM provisioning, and on-premise deployment options. Chanty offers a 50% discount for nonprofits and education, plus a 14-day money-back guarantee on paid plans.

features.title: Unified chat and task management
features.description: Convert any chat message into a task with one click, then assign, prioritize, and track it without leaving the conversation.

features.title: Large-scale video calls
features.description: Host video meetings with up to 1,000 participants and up to 49 visible video tiles at 4K quality.

features.title: Unlimited searchable history
features.description: Full-text search across all conversations, included even on the free plan.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Connect with Zapier, Google Drive, GitHub, Dropbox, Asana, and other tools teams already use.

features.title: Security & compliance options
features.description: HIPAA compliance, SSO, and two-factor authentication available on the Business plan.

features.title: Guest access
features.description: Business plan includes free guest seats per member for collaborating with external partners or clients.

pros: Generous free plan with unlimited message history for up to 5 users
Affordable per-user pricing compared to Slack and Microsoft Teams
Built-in task management removes need for a separate project tool for lightweight use
HIPAA compliance and SSO available at the standard Business tier
cons: Free plan is capped at 5 team members
Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed
Smaller third-party app ecosystem than Slack
Advanced admin controls (directory sync, SCIM) require the custom Enterprise plan

faqs.question: Is Chanty free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chanty has a free-forever plan for up to 5 team members with unlimited chat history and 20GB of storage.

faqs.question: How much does Chanty's Business plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Business plan costs $3 per user per month billed annually, or $4 per user per month billed monthly.

faqs.question: Does Chanty include task management?
faqs.answer: Yes, any chat message can be converted into a task with a single click, and tasks can be assigned, prioritized, and tracked within the app.

faqs.question: Does Chanty support video calls?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chanty supports video conferencing with up to 1,000 participants and up to 49 visible video tiles at 4K quality.

faqs.question: Is Chanty HIPAA compliant?
faqs.answer: HIPAA compliance is included on the Business plan and higher.

use_cases.title: Small business team communication
use_cases.description: Small teams use Chanty's free or low-cost Business plan as an affordable Slack alternative for daily communication.

use_cases.title: Lightweight project coordination
use_cases.description: Teams convert chat messages directly into assigned, trackable tasks without adopting a separate project management tool.

use_cases.title: Compliance-sensitive collaboration
use_cases.description: Healthcare-adjacent or security-conscious teams use Business-tier HIPAA compliance and SSO/2FA for protected communication.

enrichment.short_description: Chaport is a live chat and customer messaging platform with chatbots, a knowledge base, and multi-channel support for Facebook, Viber, Telegram, and email.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chaport is a live chat platform with chatbots and multi-channel messaging. Compare its Free, Pro, and Unlimited plans and see key features for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Chaport is a customer messaging and live chat platform launched in 2014 as a self-funded, privately held software company based in Alhambra, California. It is designed to let businesses communicate with website visitors and customers through a live chat widget while also consolidating messages from other channels — including Facebook Messenger, Viber, Telegram, and email — into a single team inbox, so support agents don't need to monitor multiple separate apps. Beyond live agent chat, Chaport includes chatbot functionality and a knowledge base with an FAQ bot, letting businesses automate answers to common questions and deflect repetitive support tickets before they reach a human agent. Other features include auto-invitations (proactive messages triggered by visitor behavior to start conversations), offline messaging when no agent is available, employee-to-employee message transferring, group chat, saved replies, pre-chat forms to collect visitor information, and detailed reporting on chat volume and agent performance. Chaport is available across web, desktop, and mobile apps, and trusted by more than 30,000 teams globally according to the company. Pricing follows a three-tier freemium structure. The Free plan includes two operator seats, unlimited chats across unlimited websites, 60 days of chat history, and basic widget customization — a usable entry point for very small teams or solo website owners. The Pro plan starts at $29 per month (billed annually) and includes four operator seats, unlimited chat history, auto-invitations, Facebook/Viber/Telegram integration, advanced widget customization, saved replies, pre-chat forms, and detailed reports. The Unlimited plan starts at $99 per month (billed annually) and removes the operator seat cap entirely, adding chatbots, the knowledge base and FAQ bot, team-based routing, assignment rules, shared saved replies, custom fields, chat ratings, advanced and on-demand reporting, and Zapier integration for connecting Chaport to other business tools. Annual billing carries roughly a 25% discount versus paying monthly, and all paid plans can be tried free for 14 days without requiring a credit card. Chaport's target customers are small and mid-sized businesses, particularly in e-commerce and online services, that want an affordable, easy-to-set-up live chat tool without needing developer resources to configure it. Its multi-channel messaging support (particularly Viber and Telegram, which are less commonly bundled by larger competitors like Intercom or Zendesk Chat) makes it a relevant option for businesses serving customer bases in Europe, Russia, and other regions where those messaging apps have high adoption. Compared to larger customer service suites, Chaport is more narrowly focused on chat and messaging rather than full helpdesk ticketing, making it a lighter-weight choice for teams whose primary need is real-time conversational support rather than a complete customer service platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Chaport is a live chat and customer messaging platform founded in 2014 and based in Alhambra, California. It lets businesses chat with website visitors in real time while also consolidating messages from Facebook, Viber, Telegram, and email into one inbox, and it adds chatbots and a knowledge base to automate common support questions.

The platform is used by more than 30,000 teams according to the company, with a focus on small and mid-sized businesses that want an affordable, quick-to-set-up alternative to larger customer service suites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core features include the live chat widget, multi-channel messaging (Facebook, Viber, Telegram, email), auto-invitations that proactively message visitors, offline messaging, group chat, and agent-to-agent chat transfers. Chatbots and a knowledge base/FAQ bot let businesses automate responses to repetitive questions.

Additional tools include saved replies, pre-chat forms, chat ratings, and both standard and on-demand reporting. Chaport is available as a web app, desktop apps, and mobile apps, and it integrates with Zapier on the top-tier plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Chaport's Free plan includes 2 operator seats, unlimited chats and websites, and 60 days of chat history. The Pro plan starts at $29/month (annual billing) with 4 operators and unlimited history, while the Unlimited plan starts at $99/month (annual billing) with unlimited operators, chatbots, and the knowledge base.
Annual billing offers roughly a 25% discount over monthly pricing, and all paid plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

features.title: Live chat widget
features.description: Real-time chat with website visitors, customizable to match brand appearance.

features.title: Multi-channel inbox
features.description: Consolidate messages from Facebook, Viber, Telegram, and email alongside live chat in one place.

features.title: Chatbots & FAQ bot
features.description: Automate answers to common questions and deflect repetitive tickets before they reach an agent.

features.title: Auto-invitations
features.description: Trigger proactive chat messages based on visitor behavior to start conversations.

features.title: Knowledge base
features.description: Self-service help content customers can search before starting a chat.

features.title: Saved replies & pre-chat forms
features.description: Speed up agent responses and collect visitor information before a conversation begins.

features.title: Reporting
features.description: Standard and on-demand reports on chat volume, response times, and agent performance.

pros: Usable free plan with 2 operators and unlimited chats/websites
Multi-channel messaging including Viber and Telegram, less common among competitors
Affordable Pro tier compared to larger customer service suites
Available on web, desktop, and mobile
cons: Free plan chat history limited to 60 days
Advanced automation (chatbots, knowledge base) only available on the top Unlimited plan
Native integrations are limited mainly to Zapier rather than a broad app marketplace
Not a full helpdesk/ticketing platform, so may need pairing with another tool for complex support operations

faqs.question: Is Chaport free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chaport has a free plan with 2 operator seats, unlimited chats and websites, and 60 days of chat history.

faqs.question: How much does Chaport cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $29/month for Pro (4 operators) and $99/month for Unlimited (unlimited operators), both billed annually.

faqs.question: Does Chaport support Facebook and Telegram messaging?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chaport consolidates Facebook Messenger, Viber, Telegram, and email alongside live chat in one inbox.

faqs.question: Does Chaport have chatbots?
faqs.answer: Yes, chatbots and a knowledge base/FAQ bot are included on the Unlimited plan.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

use_cases.title: Small business customer support
use_cases.description: Small teams use Chaport's Free or Pro plan as an affordable way to add live chat and messaging support to their website.

use_cases.title: E-commerce sales chat
use_cases.description: Online stores use auto-invitations and multi-channel messaging to engage shoppers and answer pre-sale questions in real time.

use_cases.title: Agency management of multiple client sites
use_cases.description: Agencies use Chaport across multiple websites under one account to manage chat support for several clients.

enrichment.short_description: Chargebee is a subscription billing and revenue management platform that automates recurring invoicing, payments, dunning, and usage-based monetization for SaaS companies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chargebee is a subscription billing platform for SaaS companies. See Starter, Performance, and Enterprise pricing plus key billing and revenue features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Chargebee is a subscription billing and revenue management platform founded in May 2011 in Chennai, India, by four co-founders — Krish Subramanian (CEO), Rajaraman Santhanam (COO), Saravanan KP, and Thiyagarajan Thiyagu. The company now operates with dual headquarters in San Francisco and Chennai and has grown to roughly 1,200 employees serving more than 6,500 businesses, primarily SaaS and subscription-based companies. Chargebee automates the operational complexity of recurring revenue: it handles subscription creation and management, recurring invoicing, hosted checkout pages, a self-serve customer billing portal, coupon and discount management, and integrates with more than 35 payment gateways to support customers across regions and currencies. A core value proposition is smart dunning — automated retry logic and customer communication workflows designed to recover failed or declined recurring payments, which is one of the leading causes of involuntary subscription churn. Chargebee also supports usage-based and hybrid billing models, combining flat subscription fees with metered or consumption-based charges, and has expanded this into monetization tooling aimed at AI and agentic products that need to bill for compute or API usage rather than flat seats. Pricing is structured in three tiers. The Starter plan is free for businesses with up to $250,000 in cumulative lifetime billing processed through Chargebee (not a monthly or annual reset), after which a 0.75% overage fee applies to billing processed beyond that threshold; Starter includes core recurring billing, hosted checkout, coupon management, basic gateway integrations, and webhook/API access. The Performance plan is priced at $599 per month (requiring an annual commitment, roughly $7,188/year) and is aimed at scaling subscription businesses, supporting up to $100,000 in monthly recurring revenue before the same 0.75% overage applies, and adding consolidated invoicing, smart dunning workflows, custom domain support for checkout pages, and multiple payment gateway integrations. The Enterprise plan is fully custom-priced and negotiated directly with Chargebee's sales team, adding multi-entity billing, account hierarchy management for complex organizational structures, and custom contract terms — aimed at larger companies with multiple business units, subsidiaries, or global billing entities. Chargebee integrates with major payment processors (including Stripe and PayPal among its 35+ gateways), accounting systems, and CRM platforms, and exposes a full API for teams that want to build custom billing logic on top of the platform. It is best suited for SaaS and subscription businesses that have outgrown manually managing invoicing and payment recovery in a general-purpose payment processor, and that need dunning automation, flexible pricing model support (seat-based, usage-based, or hybrid), and revenue reporting purpose-built for recurring revenue businesses. Its free Starter tier makes it accessible to early-stage startups, while the Performance and Enterprise tiers scale pricing and functionality alongside a company's billing volume and organizational complexity.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Chargebee is a subscription billing and revenue management platform founded in 2011 in Chennai, India, now with dual headquarters in San Francisco and Chennai. It automates recurring billing operations — invoicing, payments, dunning, and revenue reporting — for more than 6,500 subscription and SaaS businesses.

The platform is built to remove the operational overhead of running recurring billing manually, handling everything from checkout and payment collection to failed-payment recovery and usage-based monetization for companies with metered or consumption pricing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Chargebee supports recurring, usage-based, and hybrid billing models, hosted checkout pages and a self-serve customer portal, coupon and discount management, and integrations with more than 35 payment gateways. Smart dunning automates retrying and communicating around failed payments to reduce involuntary churn.

For larger organizations, Chargebee adds multi-entity billing and account hierarchy management, consolidated invoicing, and custom-domain checkout pages, along with a full API for teams building custom billing workflows on top of the platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The Starter plan is free for up to $250,000 in cumulative lifetime billing, after which a 0.75% overage fee applies. The Performance plan costs $599/month (annual commitment) and covers up to $100,000 in monthly recurring revenue with the same overage rate beyond that.
The Enterprise plan is custom-priced and adds multi-entity billing, account hierarchy, and negotiated contract terms for larger organizations with complex billing structures.

faqs.answer: Yes, Chargebee supports usage-based and hybrid billing models that combine flat subscription fees with metered charges.

enrichment.short_description: CharlieHR is a UK-built HR platform for small businesses, covering onboarding, absence management, and employee records, with optional recruitment and HR advice add-ons.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CharlieHR is a UK small-business HR platform for onboarding and absence tracking. Compare Core, Recruit, and Advice pricing plus key features for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CharlieHR is an HR software platform built specifically for small businesses, founded in London in 2015 (with the founding team describing operations starting from a small Whitechapel office in 2016) by co-founders including Rob O'Donovan and Ben Gateley. The company remains headquartered in London, now working with a distributed team of around 40 people across the UK, and is registered with UK Companies House as Charlie HR Ltd. The platform's core focus is replacing spreadsheets and manual admin for small-company HR teams: it centralizes employee records in a GDPR-compliant system, automates onboarding workflows for new hires, and manages absence and time-off requests with configurable leave types, manager approval flows, and real-time calendar views that sync with Google Calendar or Outlook. Employees can self-serve by requesting time off directly in the platform and viewing their own allowance usage, while managers can approve requests with one click, reducing the back-and-forth typically handled over email or spreadsheets. CharlieHR also includes automated reminders for HR-related deadlines and document storage designed to keep sensitive employee data compliant with UK/EU data protection requirements. Beyond the core HR module, CharlieHR offers two paid add-ons that require an active Core subscription and cannot be purchased standalone: Charlie Recruit, an applicant tracking system that lets small teams manage hiring pipelines without adopting separate recruitment software, and Charlie Advice, a 1-to-1 HR advisory service for businesses that want expert guidance on employment law and HR decisions rather than relying solely on self-service software. Pricing is structured by company headcount rather than a flat per-user rate: the Core HR plan starts at £20 per month for businesses with 1–4 employees and scales up to £735 per month for organizations with 250 or more employees, with new customers in the 1–4 employee band getting a 75% introductory discount for their first six months (bringing the effective starting price to roughly £5/month). The Recruit add-on starts from £55 per month and the Advice add-on starts from £179 per month, both on top of an active Core subscription; all listed prices exclude UK VAT. CharlieHR offers a 7-day free trial for prospective customers to evaluate the platform before committing. The product is primarily aimed at UK-based small businesses and startups — companies too small to justify a dedicated HR team or an enterprise HRIS platform like Workday or BambooHR, but that have outgrown tracking holidays and onboarding checklists manually. Because pricing and much of the compliance framing is UK-specific (GDPR, UK employment law guidance through Advice), CharlieHR is less commonly positioned for companies operating primarily outside the UK, and it does not include full payroll processing, which distinguishes it from all-in-one HR and payroll platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: CharlieHR is an HR software platform built for small businesses, founded in London in 2015 and still headquartered there today with a team of around 40 people across the UK. The product is designed to replace spreadsheets and manual processes for small-company HR admin, centered on onboarding, absence management, and employee records.

CharlieHR targets UK-based small businesses and startups that have outgrown manual HR tracking but are too small for an enterprise HRIS. Optional add-ons extend the core platform into recruitment and HR advisory services.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core platform includes GDPR-compliant employee records and document storage, smart onboarding workflows for new hires, and absence/time-off management with configurable leave types, manager approvals, and calendar sync to Google or Outlook. Employees can self-serve time-off requests and check their own allowance balances.

The Charlie Recruit add-on brings applicant tracking to manage hiring pipelines, and the Charlie Advice add-on connects businesses with 1-to-1 HR expertise for employment law and HR decisions — both requiring an active Core subscription.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: CharlieHR's Core HR plan is priced by company headcount, starting at £20/month for 1–4 employees and scaling to £735/month for 250+ employees, with a 75% introductory discount for the first six months for new small customers (as low as £5/month initially).
Recruit starts from £55/month and Advice starts from £179/month, both requiring an active Core subscription; all prices exclude UK VAT. A 7-day free trial is available for the Core plan.

faqs.answer: No, CharlieHR does not include full payroll processing; it focuses on onboarding, absence management, and employee records.

enrichment.short_description: Chartbrew is an open-source platform for building live dashboards and reports from databases and APIs, with an AI assistant, scheduling, and embeddable charts.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chartbrew is an open-source dashboard and reporting tool for databases and APIs. See self-hosted and cloud pricing plus key features for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Chartbrew is an open-source reporting and dashboard platform created by founder Răzvan Ilin, who built the initial version to monitor stats for his own digital products before turning it into a standalone product; the project traces to around 2018 and came out of beta in November 2020 under the trading entity Depomo Ltd. It is available as a free, self-hostable open-source application on GitHub as well as a paid cloud-hosted version at app.chartbrew.com. The platform's core function is connecting directly to data sources — relational and NoSQL databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB) as well as SaaS APIs and generic REST endpoints (including Stripe, Google Analytics, and Mailchimp) — and turning that data into charts, tables, and dashboards without requiring a separate BI or ETL layer. Users build queries either through a point-and-click query builder (selecting data sources, fields, and filters visually) or by writing raw SQL directly against connected databases, and Chartbrew includes AI-assisted query suggestions and optimization to help users write and refine SQL or MongoDB queries within the dashboard editor. Dashboards combine charts, tables, and goal-tracking widgets so teams can monitor business or product metrics at a glance, and they can be scheduled to refresh automatically, send periodic snapshot reports, or trigger alerts through Slack, email, or webhooks when metrics cross defined thresholds. Charts and full dashboards can be embedded in external websites or client-facing portals, which has made Chartbrew particularly popular with agencies that need to deliver live, branded reporting dashboards to their own clients rather than sending static reports. Pricing follows a freemium, open-core model. The self-hosted version is completely free and open-source, with users responsible for their own infrastructure (Chartbrew is commonly deployed via Docker and is also available as a DigitalOcean Marketplace image). The cloud-hosted version starts at $24 per month and includes a 14-day free trial; free-tier cloud usage is limited to core dashboard features, basic integrations, and up to 3 team members, with paid tiers unlocking more data source integrations, storage, and automation. Chartbrew is positioned as an open-source alternative to commercial BI platforms like Power BI, Tableau, and Databox, but scoped for smaller teams: it is best suited to agencies building client reporting dashboards, startups and small product teams tracking KPIs without a dedicated data/analytics engineer, and developers who want a lightweight, self-hostable BI tool that connects directly to their existing databases and SaaS APIs rather than requiring a data warehouse. It is less suited to large enterprises needing complex governance, row-level security, or massive-scale data modeling, where dedicated enterprise BI platforms remain more appropriate.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Chartbrew is an open-source dashboard and reporting platform created by founder Răzvan Ilin, originating around 2018 and publicly launching out of beta in November 2020. It is available as a free, self-hostable open-source project on GitHub and as a paid cloud-hosted service.

The platform connects directly to databases and APIs to build live charts and dashboards without a separate BI or ETL layer, and it is particularly popular with agencies and small teams that need to deliver client-facing reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Chartbrew connects to PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB databases as well as SaaS APIs like Stripe, Google Analytics, and Mailchimp, and any generic REST endpoint. A point-and-click query builder or raw SQL editor lets users shape the underlying data, with AI-assisted query suggestions helping write and optimize SQL or MongoDB queries.

Dashboards combine charts, tables, and goal-tracking widgets, with scheduled refreshes, snapshot reports, and Slack/email/webhook alerts. Charts and full dashboards can be embedded externally, which is commonly used for client-facing reporting portals.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The self-hosted, open-source version of Chartbrew is completely free, deployable via Docker or as a DigitalOcean Marketplace image. The cloud-hosted version starts at $24/month with a 14-day free trial; free cloud usage is limited to core dashboard features, basic integrations, and up to 3 team members.

Embeddable dashboards suited to client-facing reporting
cons: Free cloud tier limited to 3 team members
Better suited to small/mid-size teams than large enterprise BI deployments
Fewer prebuilt data connectors than large commercial BI platforms
Self-hosting requires basic technical setup and maintenance

faqs.question: Is Chartbrew free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted open-source version is completely free. A cloud-hosted version starts at $24/month with a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Chartbrew?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chartbrew can be self-hosted via Docker or deployed as a DigitalOcean Marketplace image.

faqs.question: What data sources does Chartbrew support?
faqs.answer: It connects to PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB databases, plus SaaS APIs like Stripe, Google Analytics, and Mailchimp, and generic REST endpoints.

faqs.question: Does Chartbrew have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes AI-assisted query suggestions and optimization for SQL and MongoDB queries.

faqs.answer: Yes, individual charts or full dashboards can be embedded externally, commonly used for client-facing reporting.

enrichment.short_description: ChartDB is a free, open-source, web-based tool that instantly visualizes database schemas as interactive ER diagrams using a single query, with no credentials leaving your machine.
enrichment.seo_title: ChartDB Review: Free Database Diagram Tool & Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ChartDB is a free, open-source tool that turns a single database query into an interactive ER diagram. See how it works, key features, and pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ChartDB is a free, open-source database schema visualization tool founded in August 2024 in Tel Aviv, Israel, by co-founders Jonathan Fishner (CEO), a software engineer with a background in data platforms who previously worked at Fiverr, and Guy Ben-Aharon (CTO), a full-stack engineer with UX experience who previously worked at Wix. The two built and launched ChartDB as an open-source MVP in under three weeks after repeatedly experiencing the same frustration on different projects: understanding an existing database's schema was slow and tedious with available tools. The launch was unusually successful for a new open-source project — it reached the front page of Hacker News within 72 hours and had accumulated more than 3,000 GitHub stars and over 20 contributors within about a month, and the company has remained fully bootstrapped with no outside funding as of mid-2025, while reporting early revenue traction. ChartDB's core mechanic is a single 'Smart Query': users run one query against their database (via chartdb.io or a self-hosted instance) that retrieves the schema as JSON, which ChartDB then renders instantly as an interactive entity-relationship (ER) diagram in the browser. Critically, the tool is designed so that no database credentials or connection strings are ever sent to ChartDB's servers — only the resulting schema JSON is used to build the diagram, which the company positions as a security and privacy advantage over tools that require direct database connections. Once a schema is imported, users can edit the diagram directly: creating, renaming, and connecting tables and relationships, with full undo/redo support, and inviting teammates to view and edit diagrams together in real time for collaborative schema design or documentation work. ChartDB also tracks schema changes over time, showing diffs and version history so teams can monitor how a database's structure evolves. For migration and documentation use cases, ChartDB can export SQL DDL scripts, including AI-driven cross-dialect export that can translate a schema from one database system to another (for example, generating PostgreSQL-compatible DDL from a MySQL schema, or migrating between SQLite and MariaDB). An AI diagram generator can also turn a natural-language description of a database into a fully editable, optimized ER diagram from scratch, useful for early-stage schema design before any actual database exists. ChartDB can be used directly at chartdb.io with no installation or account required for basic diagramming, or self-hosted for teams that want to keep all diagramming infrastructure inside their own environment. As an open-source project with no confirmed paid enterprise tier as of its research, ChartDB is positioned as a free alternative to commercial database diagramming and modeling tools like SqlDBM, aimed at developers, database administrators, and engineering teams who need to quickly understand, document, or migrate a database schema without a lengthy setup process.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ChartDB is a free, open-source database schema diagramming tool founded in August 2024 in Tel Aviv, Israel, by co-founders Jonathan Fishner and Guy Ben-Aharon. The two built and launched the project as an open-source MVP in under three weeks, reaching the front page of Hacker News within 72 hours and gaining thousands of GitHub stars shortly after launch.

The tool lets developers and database administrators instantly visualize a database's structure as an entity-relationship diagram by running a single query, without needing to install software or share database credentials with a third-party service.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ChartDB's 'Smart Query' retrieves a database's schema as JSON in one step, which is rendered instantly as an interactive ER diagram — no credentials leave the user's machine. Teams can invite collaborators to view and edit diagrams in real time, with full undo/redo and schema version history that tracks diffs over time.

For migration and documentation, ChartDB exports SQL DDL scripts, including AI-driven cross-dialect conversion (for example, MySQL to PostgreSQL). An AI diagram generator can also build a fully editable ER diagram from a natural-language description of a database, useful for early-stage schema design.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ChartDB is free and open-source, usable directly at chartdb.io with no installation or account required for core diagramming, or self-hosted for teams that want to keep the tool inside their own environment. As of its founders' most recent public updates, the company remains fully bootstrapped with no confirmed enterprise pricing tier.

features.title: Single-query schema import
features.description: Retrieve a full database schema as JSON with one 'Smart Query,' with no credentials sent to ChartDB's servers.

features.title: Instant ER diagram rendering
features.description: Automatically generate an interactive entity-relationship diagram from the imported schema.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Invite teammates to view and edit diagrams together, with full undo/redo support.

features.title: Schema version history
features.description: Track schema changes over time with diffs to monitor how the database structure evolves.

features.title: SQL export & cross-dialect migration
features.description: Export DDL scripts and use AI-driven conversion to migrate schemas between database dialects.

features.title: AI diagram generation
features.description: Generate a fully editable ER diagram from a natural-language description of a database.

pros: Completely free and open-source with no installation required for basic use
Strong privacy model since database credentials never leave the user's machine
Fast single-query schema import and instant diagram rendering
AI-assisted diagram generation and cross-dialect SQL export
cons: Young project (launched 2024) with a very small core team
No confirmed enterprise support tier or SLA
Primarily community-supported rather than backed by dedicated customer success
Advanced collaboration and version history features may be less mature than long-established paid tools

faqs.question: Is ChartDB free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ChartDB is free and open-source, usable directly at chartdb.io or self-hosted.

faqs.question: Does ChartDB store my database credentials?
faqs.answer: No, ChartDB is designed so that database credentials never leave your machine; only the resulting schema JSON is used to build the diagram.

faqs.question: Who created ChartDB?
faqs.answer: ChartDB was founded in August 2024 in Tel Aviv, Israel, by co-founders Jonathan Fishner and Guy Ben-Aharon.

faqs.question: Can ChartDB export SQL scripts?
faqs.answer: Yes, ChartDB can export SQL DDL scripts, including AI-driven conversion between different database dialects.

faqs.question: Does ChartDB support real-time collaboration?
faqs.answer: Yes, teammates can be invited to view and edit diagrams together in real time.
